name: Google Drive
description: Read and edit Google Docs, Google Sheets, and plain text files, and read Google Slides, stored in the user's Google Drive. Nothing is stored on Imperal — content is fetched live and written straight back to the source.

tools.name: skeleton_refresh_doc_reader_files
tools.description: Google Drive status — connected Google accounts, total picked files, names, sizes, extensions

tools.name: connect_google_docs
tools.description: Start Google Drive OAuth — returns an authorisation URL to open in the browser. If already connected, returns that status instead of a new URL.

tools.name: open_file_picker
tools.description: Get a link to the Google Picker where the user selects which Drive files Google Drive may access, for a specific connected account (defaults to the active one). Requires connect_google_docs first. Picked files are registered automatically into that account's own pool — call list_connected_files afterwards to check.

account.description: Which connected Google account to pick files for (email). Omit to use the active account.
account.title: Account

tools.name: register_picked_files
tools.description: Manual fallback: register files from the JSON the Picker page showed, ONLY if it displayed a copy-paste box instead of confirming automatically (rare — means the automatic path failed). Skips files already registered.

files.description: Files copied from the Google Picker page's output box (JSON with a 'files' array). Paste that exact array here.
files.title: Files

tools.name: list_connected_files
tools.description: List the Google Drive files the user has picked for Google Drive to access (name, mime type, last modified). Does not return file content. Automatically picks up files just selected in the Picker, and drops files the user deleted or unshared on Google's side.

DocFile.description: A file the user has picked through connect_google_docs' Google Picker.
DocFile.title: DocFile

tools.name: disconnect_file
tools.description: Remove a file from Google Drive's connected-files list. The Google file itself is untouched — this only stops Google Drive from tracking/reading it.

tools.name: list_accounts
tools.description: List the Google accounts connected to Google Drive — each account's email, which one is active, and how many files are in its own pool. Use when the user asks which Google accounts are connected or which one is in use.

AccountItem.description: A connected Google account and the size of its own picked-files pool.
AccountItem.title: AccountItem

tools.name: switch_account
tools.description: Change the active Google account. Subsequent file listing, picking, reading and editing use this account's own pool of picked files until switched again.

account.description: Which connected Google account — its email address (from list_accounts).
account.title: Account

tools.name: disconnect_account
tools.description: Disconnect a Google account from Google Drive and forget its pool of picked files. Nothing is deleted in Google Drive itself; the OAuth grant can be fully revoked by the user at myaccount.google.com/permissions.

account.description: Which connected Google account — its email address (from list_accounts).
account.title: Account

tools.name: read_text_file
tools.description: Read a file from the user's Drive that is NOT a Google Doc/Sheet — plain text, PDF, DOCX, XLSX, PPTX, or similar. Text is always extracted first (never raw bytes). Returns line-numbered text. Omit limit to read the whole file.

limit.description: Max number of lines to return. Omit to read to the end.
limit.title: Limit

return_schema.description: A line-numbered window of a text file or document's content.
return_schema.title: TextWindow

tools.name: write_text_file
tools.description: Overwrite a plain text file's entire content in the user's Drive. Only works for genuinely text-based files (not PDF/DOCX/etc — those are read-only). Changes the real file immediately.

file_id.description: File ID (Google Doc or plain text file).
file_id.title: File Id

content.description: New full content — replaces everything currently in the file.
content.title: Content

tools.name: search_in_text_file
tools.description: Search a Drive file (PDF/DOCX/XLSX/PPTX/text) for a substring — text is extracted first, then searched. Returns matching lines with line numbers.

query.description: Text to search for (case-insensitive by default).
query.title: Query

tools.name: read_document_text
tools.description: Read a Google Doc's text content. Returns line-numbered text (like a source file). Omit limit to read the whole document — use offset/limit only for very long documents.

limit.description: Max number of lines to return. Omit to read to the end.
limit.title: Limit

return_schema.description: A line-numbered window of a text file or document's content.
return_schema.title: TextWindow

tools.name: search_in_document
tools.description: Search a Google Doc's text for a substring. Returns matching lines with line numbers — use this instead of read_document_text when you only need to locate something in a long document.

query.description: Text to search for (case-insensitive by default).
query.title: Query

tools.name: get_document_stats
tools.description: Get exact character/word/paragraph counts for a Google Doc, computed precisely (not estimated). Use this for questions like 'how many words/characters does this document have' instead of counting from read_document_text output.

tools.name: replace_text_in_document
tools.description: Find-and-replace exact text in a Google Doc — changes the real document immediately. Fails with a clear error if find_text has no matches (check the exact wording with read_document_text first).

tools.name: append_text_to_document
tools.description: Add text to the end of a Google Doc — changes the real document immediately.

text.description: Text to append at the end of the document.
text.title: Text

tools.name: overwrite_document_text
tools.description: Replace a Google Doc's entire content with new text — changes the real document immediately. Use for a full rewrite; use replace_text_in_document for a targeted edit instead.

file_id.description: File ID (Google Doc or plain text file).
file_id.title: File Id

content.description: New full content — replaces everything currently in the file.
content.title: Content

tools.name: get_spreadsheet_info
tools.description: List a Google Sheet's tab names and dimensions (rows/columns). Call this before read_spreadsheet_range/write_spreadsheet_range if you don't already know the sheet name — there's no way to guess it otherwise.

return_schema.description:
Sheet names + dimensions — needed before a range can be addressed by
name, since there is no way to guess a sheet's title otherwise.
return_schema.title: SpreadsheetInfo

tools.name: read_spreadsheet_range
tools.description: Read a range of cells from a Google Sheet (A1 notation, e.g. 'Sheet1!A1:D10', or a bare sheet name for the whole sheet). Returns the raw values as a 2D array. Use get_spreadsheet_info first if you don't know the sheet name.

cell_range.description: A1 notation range, e.g. 'Sheet1!A1:D10', or just a sheet name for the whole sheet.
cell_range.title: Cell Range

tools.name: write_spreadsheet_range
tools.description: Write a 2D array of values into a Google Sheet range (A1 notation) — changes the real spreadsheet immediately, overwriting only the given range.

tools.name: aggregate_spreadsheet_range
tools.description: Compute an exact sum/count/average/min/max over a Google Sheet range, calculated in code — not estimated by reading the dumped values. Use this instead of read_spreadsheet_range for questions like 'what's the total of column B'.

operation.description: Exact computation to run over the range's numeric cells (count counts all non-empty cells, not just numeric ones).
operation.title: Operation

tools.name: read_presentation_text
tools.description: Read a Google Slides presentation's text content (all slides, exported as plain text by Google Drive). Read-only — Slides editing isn't supported. Returns line-numbered text. Omit limit to read the whole thing.

limit.description: Max number of lines to return. Omit to read to the end.
limit.title: Limit

return_schema.description: A line-numbered window of a text file or document's content.
return_schema.title: TextWindow

tools.name: search_in_presentation
tools.description: Search a Google Slides presentation's text for a substring. Returns matching lines with line numbers.

query.description: Text to search for (case-insensitive by default).
query.title: Query

secrets.name: google_client_id
secrets.description: Shared Google OAuth Client ID for Google Drive (developer-owned; one OAuth app for all users)

secrets.name: google_client_secret
secrets.description: Shared Google OAuth Client Secret for Google Drive (developer-owned)

secrets.name: google_picker_api_key
secrets.description: Google API Key restricted to Picker API for the picker.html static page (HTTP referrer restricted)

secrets.name: picker_hmac_secret
secrets.description: HMAC key shared with doc-extractor-service for the Picker OAuth token handoff
